title: DSDS Token and Token Group Definitions
description: Docs for individual design tokens and hierarchical token groups. Token values, aliases, and type information live in the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) file — DSDS documents the purpose, guidelines, and organization only. Token groups are flexible organizational units: a full token collection, a token family (e.g., all color tokens), or a sub-family (e.g., a single hue family). Groups can hold tokens, nested groups, or both, forming a hierarchy that mirrors how design systems organize tokens. Tokens and token groups omit the display `name` property — the `identifier` itself serves as the display label. Description and status are optional to keep things terse at scale. The `metadata` object carries optional metadata fields (status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, links).

token.description: Docs for a single design token: identifier, type, use cases, guidelines, and accessibility considerations. The display `name` property is absent — the `identifier` itself serves as the display label. Description and status are optional. The `metadata` object carries optional metadata fields (status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, links). When a token sits inside a token group and omits its own status metadata field, the parent group's status applies.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token.

identifier.description: The token identifier. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions that use dots, slashes, or other separators (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary'). Also serves as the human-readable display label.

tokenType.description: The type of token aligned with the DTCG spec (e.g., 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'cubicBezier', 'shadow').

description.description: What this token represents and when to use it. Optional — keep terse at scale. CommonMark supported.

source.description: Pointer to the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this token's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this token within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text.primary').

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Tokens accept the general kinds only (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured documentation for agent (AI/LLM) consumption only — a partitioned space accepting the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation constraints, disambiguation between confusable entities, machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing documentation. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.

tokenGroup.description: A group of related design tokens. Token groups are flexible organizational units: a full collection, a family (e.g., all color tokens), a sub-family (e.g., a single hue with its grade scale), or any other logical grouping. Groups can nest to form a deep hierarchy. A top-level group might represent 'color' and hold child groups for 'color-text', 'color-background', and 'color-border', each with individual tokens. This recursive structure mirrors how real design systems organize tokens into families and sub-families. The display `name` property is absent — the `identifier` itself serves as the display label. Description and status are optional. The `metadata` object carries optional metadata fields (status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links). When a child omits its own status metadata field, the parent group's status applies.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token-group.

identifier.description: The token group identifier. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text', 'color.text', 'color/text'). Also serves as the human-readable display label.

description.description: What this token group covers and how it is organized. Optional — keep terse at scale. CommonMark supported.

tokenType.description: When all tokens in this group share the same DTCG token type, declare it here to avoid repetition on each child. Each token in children MAY override this value. Common values: 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'number', 'shadow'.

source.description: Pointer to the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this group's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this group within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text').

children.description: An ordered array of tokens and/or nested token groups inside this group. Each item MUST be either a token or a tokenGroup. Order matters — tools SHOULD preserve it for display, since it often shows a meaningful progression (e.g., lightest to darkest, or smallest to largest). Children inherit tokenType and status from this group when they omit their own values.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token group, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the general kinds only (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). Use this array for docs that apply to the group as a whole. Place docs for individual tokens on the token inside `children`.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured documentation for agent (AI/LLM) consumption only — a partitioned space accepting the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation constraints, disambiguation between confusable entities, machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing documentation. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.
